Appearance
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
List of squads for men's international team for FIFA World Cup 2026

The 2026 FIFA World Cup is an international football tournament which will be held in Canada, Mexico, and United States, from June 11 to July 19, 2026. The 48 national teams involved in the tournament will be required to register a squad of up to 26 players, including three goalkeepers. Only players in these squads will eligible to take part in the tournament.[1]

A provisional release list of between 35 and 55 players per national team had to be submitted to FIFA by May 11, 2026, one month prior to the opening match of the tournament. The release lists will not be made public by FIFA. From the preliminary squad, the final list of at least 23 and at most 26 players per national team will be submitted to FIFA by June 1, ten days prior to the opening match of the tournament.[2] In the event that a player on the submitted squad list suffered from an injury or illness prior to his team's first match of the tournament, that player could be replaced at any time up to 24 hours before their first match. The team doctor and the FIFA General Medical Officer had to both confirm that the injury or illness was severe enough to prevent the player from participating in the tournament. Replacement players did not need to be limited to the preliminary list.[1]

The position listed for each player is per the official squad list published by FIFA. The age listed for each player is as of June 11, 2026, the first day of the tournament. The numbers of caps and goals listed for each player do not include any matches played after the start of the tournament. The club listed is the club for which the player last played a competitive match prior to the tournament.[note 1] The nationality for each club reflects the national association (not the league) to which the club is affiliated. A flag is included for coaches who are of a different nationality to their team.

Group A

[edit]

Czech Republic

[edit]

Coach: Miroslav Koubek

The Czech Republic announced a 54-man preliminary squad on May 12.[3] The squad was reduced to 29 players on May 21.[4] Their final squad will be announced on May 31.[5]

Statistics

[edit]
This section does not cite any sources. Please help improve this section by adding citations to reliable sources. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. (May 2026) (Learn how and when to remove this message)

Age

[edit]

Coaches

[edit]
  • Oldest: Dick Advocaat (78 years, 257 days)
  • Youngest: Julian Nagelsmann (38 years, 323 days)

Players

[edit]

[note 2]

  • Oldest: Craig Gordon (43 years, 162 days)
  • Youngest: Lennart Karl (18 years, 109 days)

Coach representation by country

[edit]

Coaches in bold represented their own country.

Number of coaches from that country Country Coaches
6  Argentina Lionel Scaloni, Gustavo Alfaro (Paraguay), Mauricio Pochettino (United States), Sebastián Beccacece (Ecuador), Marcelo Bielsa (Uruguay), Néstor Lorenzo (Colombia)
5  France Didier Deschamps, Sébastien Migné (Haiti), Sabri Lamouchi (Tunisia), Rudi Garcia (Belgium), Sébastien Desabre (DR Congo)
4  Spain Luis de la Fuente, Julen Lopetegui (Qatar), Roberto Martínez (Portugal), Thomas Christiansen (Panama)
3  Italy Carlo Ancelotti (Brazil), Vincenzo Montella (Turkey), Fabio Cannavaro (Uzbekistan)
 Germany Julian Nagelsmann, Ralf Rangnick (Austria), Thomas Tuchel (England)
2   Switzerland Murat Yakin, Vladimir Petković (Algeria)
 Morocco Mohamed Ouahbi, Jamal Sellami (Jordan)
 Australia Tony Popovic, Graham Arnold (Iraq)
 Netherlands Ronald Koeman, Dick Advocaat (Curaçao)
 England Graham Potter (Sweden), Darren Bazeley (New Zealand)
1  Mexico Javier Aguirre
 Belgium Hugo Broos (South Africa)
 South Korea Hong Myung-bo
 Czech Republic Miroslav Koubek
 Bosnia and Herzegovina Sergej Barbarez
 Scotland Steve Clarke
 United States Jesse Marsch (Canada)
 Ivory Coast Emerse Faé
 Japan Hajime Moriyasu
 Egypt Hossam Hassan
 Iran Amir Ghalenoei
 Cape Verde Bubista
 Greece Georgios Donis (Saudi Arabia)
 Senegal Pape Thiaw
 Norway Ståle Solbakken
 Croatia Zlatko Dalić
 Portugal Carlos Queiroz (Ghana)

Notes

[edit]
  1. ^ This is the club a player was last able to play for during the previous season in the event a player did not play a competitive match.
  2. ^ Considering only players who are on the final lists already released.

  4. ^ "Reprezentace před MS: širší nominace se třemi nováčky i navrátilcem Hložkem" [Representation before the World Cup: extended nomination with three newcomers and returnee Hložek] (in Czech). Football Association of the Czech Republic. May 21, 2026. Retrieved May 21, 2026.
  5. ^ "Reprezentace oznámí konečnou nominaci na MS po utkání s Kosovem" [The national team will announce its final squad for the World Cup after the match against Kosovo]. ČeskéNoviny.cz (in Czech). May 14, 2026. Retrieved May 19, 2026.
  6. ^ "La Selección Nacional de México anuncia Prelista para el Mundial 2026" [The Mexican National Team announces its preliminary roster for the 2026 World Cup] (in Spanish). Mexican Football Federation. May 12, 2026. Retrieved May 12, 2026.

"🇮🇶 | برنامجُ تحضيراتِ منتخبنا الوطني لكأس العالم 2026 🌍🏆يتضمنُ المعسكراتِ التدريبية والمبارياتِ التحضيرية ضمن الاستعداداتِ للمشاركةِ في نهائيات كأس العالم.| Iraq National Team preparations schedule for the 2026 FIFA World Cup 🌍🏆Including training camps and friendly matches as part of the team's preparations for the World Cup finals". Instagram. Retrieved May 18, 2026.
  50. ^ "Official: Norway's squad for the World Cup". ca.sports.yahoo.com. May 21, 2026. Retrieved May 21, 2026.
  51. ^ "Mondial 2026: Pape Thiaw publie sa liste" [2026 World Cup: Pape Thiaw releases his squad list] (in French). Senegalese Football Federation. May 21, 2026. Retrieved May 21, 2026.
  52. ^ "Coupe du monde 2026 : 10 joueurs locaux dans la liste élargie de Petkovic" [2026 World Cup: 10 local players on Petković's preliminary roster]. Algerie360 (in French). May 11, 2026. Retrieved May 13, 2026.
  53. ^ "Selección Mayor: Prelista de convocados" [Senior National Team: Preliminary squad list]. Argentine Football Association. May 11, 2026. Retrieved May 11, 2026.
  54. ^ "Ralf Rangnick nennt WM-Kader" [Ralf Rangnick names World Cup squad] (in German). Austrian Football Association. May 18, 2026. Archived from the original on May 18, 2026. Retrieved May 18, 2026.
  55. ^ "إعلان القائمة الأولية لمنتخب النشامى تأهبا لمواجهتي سويسرا وكولومبيا وديا" [Announcement of the preliminary roster for the Nashama national team ahead of the friendly matches against Switzerland and Colombia] (in Arabic). Jordan Football Association. May 17, 2026. Retrieved May 18, 2026.
  56. ^ "Selección Colombia Masculina de Mayores: Lista previa de Convocados para la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026" [Colombia Men's National Team: Preliminary squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup] (in Spanish). Colombian Football Federation. May 14, 2026. Retrieved May 14, 2026.
  57. ^ Ariza, Diego (May 14, 2026). "La selección Colombia ya tendría fecha para presentar la lista de 26 convocados al Mundial 2026: habría evento especial" [The Colombian national team reportedly has a date set to unveil its 26-man squad for the 2026 World Cup—and a special event is said to be in the works]. Infobae (in Spanish). Retrieved May 15, 2026.
  58. ^ "Liste officielle" [Official squad] (in French). Congolese Association Football Federation. May 18, 2026. Retrieved May 18, 2026 – via Instagram.
  59. ^ "Desabre names fearless DR Congo squad for historic World Cup return". Confederation of African Football. May 18, 2026. Archived from the original on May 18, 2026. Retrieved May 18, 2026.
  60. ^ "Communiqué officiel" [Official communication] (in French). Congolese Association Football Federation. May 20, 2026. Retrieved May 20, 2026 – via Instagram.
  61. ^ Atkinson, Mark (May 21, 2026). "Hibs defender Rocky Bushiri opens up on World Cup injury heartache and makes vow". Edinburgh Evening News. Archived from the original on May 21, 2026. Retrieved May 21, 2026.
  62. ^ "Os escolhidos para o Mundial 2026" [The selections for the 2026 World Cup] (in Portuguese). Portuguese Football Federation. May 19, 2026. Archived from the original on May 19, 2026. Retrieved May 19, 2026.
  63. ^ "Roberto Martinez names Portugal World Cup squad with symbolic 'plus one' for Dioga Jota". The Irish Times. May 19, 2026. Archived from the original on May 19, 2026. Retrieved May 19, 2026. Martinez confirmed that fourth-choice goalkeeper Ricardo Velho of Genclerbirligi Ankara will travel with the squad but can only be added to the official 26-man list in the event of an injury to one of the three registered goalkeepers.
  64. ^ "Training Camp May 6–26: Uzbekistan Squad" (in Uzbek and English). Uzbekistan Football Association. May 5, 2026. Retrieved May 5, 2026 – via Facebook.
  65. ^ "Zlatko Dalić objavio popis igrača za Svjetsko prvenstvo u Kanadi, Meksiku i SAD-u" [Zlatko Dalić announces list of players for the World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the USA] (in Croatian). Croatian Football Federation. May 18, 2026. Archived from the original on May 18, 2026. Retrieved May 18, 2026.
  66. ^ Veevers, Nicholas (May 8, 2026). "England's World Cup squad announcement date revealed". The Football Association. Retrieved May 10, 2026.
  67. ^ Fittipaldi, Juan (May 11, 2026). "Panamá tiene todo definido: Thomas Christiansen toma una tajante postura sobre la prelista para el Mundial 2026" [Panama has made its decision: Thomas Christiansen takes a firm stance on the preliminary roster for the 2026 World Cup]. Fútbol Centroamérica (in Spanish). Retrieved May 13, 2026.
